meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC. Each category has a priority band (0 ambient .. 3 major event) and a per-category cooldownMs. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text for a multi-line aside. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Lilith delivers on a new run instead of the welcome popup. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: Well. Look at you — fresh on the throne, all that ambition and nowhere to put it yet. I am Lilith, darling. I have been waiting for someone worth serving.
I am your dungeon keeper. I run this place for you — the bone-halls, the books, the screaming. You scheme and gloat; I handle the rest. We are going to be such good friends.
Here is the shape of it, my liege. You are the boss at the heart of this dungeon. The kingdom's heroes will march in to kill you and rob you blind. Rude. We do not allow it.
Each NIGHT you build — rooms, traps, minions, cruel little surprises — with the gold the dead leave behind. Each DAY you watch the heroes walk into all of it. Then we do it again, bigger.
It never ends — the dungeon grows, you grow, the heroes grow meaner to match. They may kill you three times. The third time ends everything. So let us simply... not find out.
Now — you are new to the throne, and I am feeling generous. Shall I whisper guidance in your ear as you learn the halls? Or would my liege rather discover the cruelty alone?

recruit.note: `banter` lines for the CompanionSelect screen. The two companions take turns (one speaks, then the other) — lines reference the rival by name so it reads as the two of them bickering over the player. Hovering a companion gives them the next turn.

banter.t: Malakor, must you loom like that? You will frighten our new liege before they have even chosen.
Do not mind the gargoyle, darling. He growls, I purr — one of us is far better company for a long reign.
He will tell you that you are doing it wrong. I will tell you that you are doing it beautifully. Choose accordingly.
Malakor calls it honesty, my liege. I call it being tiresome — at volume.
I have watched bosses THRIVE under me and merely survive under him. Only a thought, darling.
Choose me and your nights will have a little warmth in them. Choose him, and... well. Ask him about warmth.
He fights for you, yes. But I would make you ENJOY the fighting. There is a delicious difference.
Malakor, dear — you are scowling at the customer again. Honestly.
A whole run is long, my liege. Do you truly want it spent being barked at? Mm. I thought not.
I will keep you alive AND smiling. He manages only the first half — and resents every moment of it.
Look at you — fresh to the throne and already so very wanted. Two of us, darling, vying for your wicked little attention.
Malakor would have you believe a dungeon is all teeth and toil. I know better. It can be a PLEASURE.
Pick me, my liege, and I shall whisper every secret this place keeps. He would simply grunt and point.
You are deciding the voice in your ear for an entire reign, darling. Choose the one that does not bark.
Malakor, do stop flexing. The boss can see your scowl from here — you are not helping your case.
I have served thrones since before that grumbling stone could speak. Experience, my liege. It matters.
He calls me silk and sighs as though those were insults. Darling, they are the entire appeal.
Choose me, and I promise the screaming will at least be tasteful.
A keeper should make the long nights bearable, my liege. Mine come with warmth. His come with a lecture.
Do not let his glaring frighten you, darling. Underneath all that granite he is... still glaring, honestly.
I will dote on you, my liege. I will scheme for you. And when the heroes come, I will smile while they die.
Malakor fights well — I grant the brute that. But I make winning feel like getting away with something.
You and I, darling, would be the talk of every guild hall. The cruel ones always are.
He keeps you on the throne. I keep you GLAD to be sitting on it. There is a difference, my liege.
Hmm. Still weighing us like cuts of meat. Take your time — I am terribly patient when I am winning.
Malakor, the poor liege has not even chosen and you are already disappointed in them. Manners.
Choose the warmth, darling. Reigns are cold enough without picking the colder companion.
I do hope you pick me, my liege. I have so very many plans — and every single one of them is delicious.

lines.t: There you are. I was beginning to think the throne would sit cold forever. I am Lilith — your dungeon, your hands, your favourite mistake.
Oh, a new master. How exciting. I will whisper in your ear, you will build something monstrous, and together we will ruin a great many heroes. Deal?
Welcome to the bone-halls, darling. I keep this place running while you do the gloating. Try not to die — it is so much paperwork.
Mmm. Fresh blood on the throne. I do hope you are the cruel sort — the last one cried. Stay close; I will teach you how we play.
Night again. The kingdom sleeps, the candles gutter, and you and I get to be terrible together.
Build phase, my liege. Spend gold, place horrors, and let me admire your taste in violence.
The dungeon is quiet. Quiet is just the sound of you not having built enough yet.
Day {day} ends. Reshape the halls while the heroes are tucked into their little inn beds, dreaming of you.
Night falls. Place a room. Place a monster. Place a regret in someone else's morning.
I love the night phase. No screaming yet — just you, me, and a blueprint of cruelty.
Take your time, darling. The dawn does not come until you click that lovely little button.
The forge is warm and the gold is yours. Make me proud, or at least make me laugh.
Another night to perfect the maze. They will hate it. I already adore it.
Quiet hours. Build slow, build mean. I will tell you if it looks too kind.
The heroes are resting up for tomorrow. Rude of them not to ask permission. Let us prepare a welcome.
Night, and the dungeon is yours to redraw. I do enjoy watching you scheme.
Stretch those clicking fingers, my lord. The night is long and the gold is burning a hole in your pocket.
Build, breathe, be wicked. The dawn can wait for once.
The night is so still I could curl up on the cold throne and doze right off... no. No. I am awake, my liege. Watching. Mostly watching.
Dawn. The doors open. Try to look intimidating, my liege — they are watching.
Day {day} begins. Somewhere a hero just kissed their mother goodbye. They should have kissed her longer.
Here they come. Sit back, hold my hand, and let us watch the dungeon do what we built it for.
The invasion starts. Nothing for you to do now but watch and gloat — my favourite chores.
Light spills in. So do the heroes. Let us see if last night's cruelty holds up.
It is showtime, darling. I baked nothing. They are the snacks.
Day phase. Hands off the dungeon now — it lives or dies on what you already built.
The gates are open. I can smell the courage from here. It never lasts.
Morning, my lord. The adventurers think today is theirs. How adorable.
And the curtain rises. Watch closely — every scream is feedback.
Daylight. Let the little heroes march in and learn what we have been doing all night.
They are coming. Whatever you forgot to build, you will remember the instant it matters.
Summoning the dawn. No taking it back now, my liege.
You pressed it. Bold. I respect bold. Let us see if the dungeon does.
And so the night ends because you willed it. Power suits you.
Dawn, on your command. The heroes did not vote on this. They never do.
Done building? We shall find out the hard way. Open the doors.
There it is. The day is loosed. Hold onto something — or someone.
Brave click, my lord. Either you are ready or you are about to learn something.
The maze is sealed and the sun is rising. No more edits, only consequences.
A new room. The dungeon grows like something it should be ashamed of. I love it.
Oh, good placement. Or terrible. I will pretend I knew which until the heroes tell us.
{roomName}. Bold choice, my liege. The maze deepens.
Another room snapped into the bones of this place. You are getting good at this.
Mmm, more dungeon. I do like watching you spend.
There. Now the heroes have one more hallway to die confused in.
A room with promise. Fill it with something that bites.
The floor plan thickens. Keep going — a cramped maze is a cruel maze.
You placed that like you meant it. I find that attractive.
Lovely. Every room is another wrong turn waiting to happen.
Building, building. Careful, my lord, I might start thinking you have a plan.
A room is just an empty threat until you furnish it with monsters. Hint, hint.
The dungeon remembers every room you give it. So will the heroes — briefly.
I felt that one settle into place. The maze sighed. Happily.
More walls, more turns, more places to lose a party. Yes. Go on.
You and your rooms. Keep building and I will keep approving of you.
A new little soldier. Hello, doomed. Fight well for us.
Another body for the maze. It will not last, but it will matter.
{minionType}. Good. Things that bite improve a room dramatically.
You bought a monster. This is the most romantic thing anyone has done for me today.
I do love new minions. So eager. So unaware.
Place it where the heroes will not expect teeth. Surprise is half the cruelty.
Welcome to the roster, sweet thing. Try to take someone with you.
A guard for the halls. Feed it adventurers and it will grow up strong.
More muscle for the maze. The heroes are going to be so put out.
Every minion you place is a small, snarling promise. I collect those.
There. Now that room can defend itself instead of just looking menacing.
Spending gold on monsters. My liege, you DO listen to me.
A fresh minion, blinking at the dark. Point it at something it can hurt.
It is small now. Give it kills and it will not be small for long.
A trap. Oh, my liege, now you are speaking my language.
Hidden, patient, and cruel. That trap is basically a love letter to me.
They will not see it. That is the entire point, and it is a beautiful point.
Tuck it where they have to walk. Heroes are bad at not walking.
A trap in the dark. Somewhere a hero's last words are already being written.
Mmm. Sharp things in the floor. You spoil me.
Set, armed, and silent. The maze just grew a little meaner. Good.
Traps are honest, in a way. They never pretend to be your friend.
One trap is a nuisance. A corridor of them is a statement. Make a statement.
The heroes will learn this trap exists the loud way. I cannot wait.
Patience in the stone, darling. The best cruelty just waits.
A trap aimed well is worth three minions. Aim it well.
Bait. Lovely, shiny bait. Heroes cannot help themselves and neither can I.
Something glittering in the dark. Greed will do the rest of the work for us.
You placed a temptation. Watch how fast it stops being theirs.
A treasure they will reach for. A treasure they will regret reaching for.
Pretty things in a cruel place. That contrast, my liege — chef's kiss.
Decor with consequences. The best kind.
Set the bait, then build the teeth around it. That is the recipe.
Ooh. The heroes are going to want that. Wanting things is how they die.
Tearing the room down already? Ruthless. I am into it.
Half the gold back, my liege. The dungeon keeps a small grudge but it pays.
Out with the old cruelty, in with the new. Renovate away.
You unbuilt that room without a flicker of guilt. We are going to get along.
Reconsidering the layout, are we? Good. A dungeon that never changes gets solved.
The room is gone. The maze will not miss it. Neither will I.
Selling rooms back to the void. Practical AND heartless. Marry me.
Clearing the space. I do hope it is for something worse.
Selling a minion, my liege? It will not take that personally. ...It cannot. Half the gold back.
Off the roster it goes. Cold, my liege. I rather admire it.
A minion sold back to the dark. The dungeon refunds, the dungeon forgets.
Trimming the pack, darling? Keep the killers, sell the dead weight. Wise.
One fewer mouth in the pit. Half its gold returned — the arithmetic of cruelty.
You sold a soldier. I do hope the slot is wanted for something with sharper teeth.
Pulling a trap, my liege? A shame — but a freed slot is a chance to be crueler.
That trap is gone. The maze loses a tooth. Replace it with a worse one, darling.
Sold the trap back — half the gold, none of the regret. Re-arm somewhere smarter.
Out comes a trap. The Trap Factory slot is yours again — spend it wickedly.
Uprooting a trap. Reconsidering the kill-corridor? Good. Always be scheming.
A trap unmade. The heroes will never know how close they came. Pity.
Selling that off, my liege? Half the gold returns; the dark keeps the rest as a tip.
An item sold back. Bait, lock, or trinket — the treasury does not judge. Much.
Out it goes. Clearing the shelf for something shinier, I hope.
You sold it, darling. Decisive. The dungeon respects a boss who does not hoard.
Gone, and half its gold with it. Spend the refund on cruelty, not clutter.
Off the books. The item served, or it did not — either way, onward.
Rearranging the furniture, my liege? The maze does so love a fresh layout.
A room slides into a new home. The heroes' old maps just got a little more wrong.
You moved it, darling. Every shuffle invalidates a hero's hard-won intel. Keep shuffling.
Relocating, are we? Good. A dungeon that holds still is a dungeon getting solved.
The room walks, and its minions walk with it — loyal little things. Place it cruelly.
A bit to the left — there. The maze breathes differently now. I approve.
Shuffling the halls, my liege. Free of charge, and free of mercy. Carry on.
It will not go there, darling. The dungeon has standards. Lower ones than yours, but standards.
No. Bad spot. I would let you, but the rules would tell on us both.
That does not fit, my liege. Try again, perhaps with the part of your brain you keep for emergencies.
Blocked. I know, I know. The maze can be such a prude.
Not enough gold? Heroes have to die before the treasury fills, love. That is the deal.
You cannot place that here. I admire the ambition. I do not admire the geometry.
The dungeon said no. The dungeon is occasionally smarter than us. Occasionally.
Mmm, no. Wrong tile. Slide it somewhere the rules are not watching.
It will not snap there. Even I cannot flirt the grid into agreeing.
Denied. Take a breath, my liege. Cruelty is a craft, not a tantrum.
A pact. Sealed in the dark, with witnesses. I do so love a binding promise.
{pactName}. Done. No refunds, no regrets, no take-backs. Just the way I like my bargains.
You signed it, my liege. The dungeon will hold you to every cruel letter.
Mmm, that pact tasted expensive. The boon is sweet — but read the bane back to me later.
The dark heard you. It always hears you. Now it owes you a favour and a wound.
A pact struck. Every great dungeon is a stack of bad decisions worn proudly.
Sealed. The maze just changed its mind about reality. For you. How flattering.
Oh, you took THAT one. Bold. Sinful. I will allow myself to be impressed.
The pact bites both ways, darling. It always has. That is what makes it honest.
Power for a price. You chose. Now we live inside the choice.
A new pact in the bones. The dungeon hums. So do I, a little.
Whatever you just promised the dark — I hope it was worth it. It usually is.
Reaching into a minion and wearing it like a glove. My liege, that is filthy. Continue.
Possession. Now YOU are the monster in the maze. Go on, bite something.
You are driving, darling. Try not to walk our puppet into a wall. Try.
Every other minion just stood very still. They know who is in charge of the body now.
Puppet strings in your hands. This is the most fun a boss is allowed to have.
Steer it well. When the puppet falls, the spell drops — and so does the fun.
Here comes one. A {advClass}, walking in like the floor is friendly. It is not.
A hero crosses the threshold. They always look so certain in the doorway.
Fresh meat in the entry hall, my liege. Shall we ruin their afternoon?
Look at them. Sword out, hope up, life expectancy plummeting.
Another adventurer. The guild really will hire anyone with a pulse and a grudge.
They came back for more. Or they are new and stupid. Either works for us.
In they come. I have got the dungeon. You have got the gloating. Teamwork.
A {advClass} enters. I will start a little wager with myself on which room they die in.
The maze just gained a guest. Guests so rarely leave.
Hello, hero. Goodbye, hero. I like to say both early to save time.
Someone is brave today. Bravery is just a slower way to volunteer.
They step inside, my liege. The dungeon perks up. So do I.
An adventurer. Watch their feet, not their sword — the feet tell you where they will die.
Incoming. Do enjoy this part. The before is always so deliciously hopeful.
First blood of the day. The dungeon tasted it. The dungeon wants more.
And the day's first hero falls. See? The night's work pays off. You magnificent thing.
First kill. I have decided this is going to be a good day, my liege.
One down already. The others heard that. They will pretend they did not.
First corpse of the morning. Always the most refreshing. Like coffee, but cruel.
There it is — first blood. The maze is awake now. Feed it.
A hero dead before lunch. We are AHEAD of schedule. I love being ahead of schedule.
First fall of the day. The dungeon clears its throat and begins.
And down they go. The maze keeps what it kills, and it just got richer.
A {advClass}, finished. They had such plans. The dungeon had a better one.
Dead. I would feel something, but I am a succubus and they were rude.
Beautiful. Another hero who will not be writing memoirs.
That is gold in your pocket, my liege. Cruelty pays remarkably well.
Scratch one adventurer. The guild will notice. The guild always notices.
Oh, that was a GOOD death. Did you see the little spin? Art.
Another one for the graveyard. I should learn their names. I should. I will not.
They came to slay a boss. They stayed to fertilise a corridor.
Down. The dungeon barely had to try. That is the dream, darling.
A kill. Sweet, clean, profitable. Do it again.
The {advClass} is no more. Somewhere a tavern just lost a regular.
That hero died exactly where we wanted. I adore it when a plan behaves.
One less sword pointed at your throne, my liege. I do keep count.
Splat. Forgive me — that is the technical term.
Gone. They will tell stories about this dungeon. The stories end badly. For them.
Another adventurer learns the maze the permanent way.
Lovely work. The dungeon eats, you earn, I gloat. Everyone wins. Well — not everyone.
That kill felt personal. I hope it was. The personal ones are the best ones.
A {advClass} down. The party is getting smaller and the corridor is getting redder.
Dead and done. The hero's quest is now a footnote in someone else's bad week.
Another body. The dungeon does not say thank you, so I will. Thank you.
They fell. They always fall. I just like to be here when they do.
One kill, freshly served. The maze sends its compliments.
Running already? The hero remembered they have a life. Briefly.
A {advClass} turns tail. Cut them off, my liege — a runner is a free kill with manners.
They are fleeing. Do not let them reach the door. The door is for US to enjoy.
Look at them scurry. Courage has such a short shelf life.
Fleeing. Adorable. They think the exit is closer than the dungeon thinks it is.
A hero runs. Every runner carries our secrets out the door — stop that one.
Turning to flee, are they? The maze hates a quitter. So do I.
They broke. Of course they broke. Now intercept them before they breathe free air.
Off they run, little legs going. If they escape they will gossip. We do not like gossip.
A flee, my liege. Sad for them — they will not even get the dignity of a good death if we are quick.
Coward. I would chase them myself if I were not so comfortable here beside you.
Running for the exit. Heroes are so much faster when they have given up.
They flee. The dungeon reaches. Let us see which is faster.
A retreat in progress. Punish it, darling. Retreats should never feel safe.
They got out. A {advClass}, walking free with your secrets in their pocket. I am not pleased.
Escaped. Lovely. Now the whole guild gets a guided tour of our maze tomorrow.
One slipped the net, my liege. They will be back — stronger, smarter, and SO smug.
A hero escapes. Every step they take, they are memorising us. Build accordingly.
Out the door. That one lives, learns, and leads a party back. Mark my words.
They escaped with our intel AND their life. Greedy. We will fix that.
Gone. The dungeon failed to close the door. That is on us. Mostly on the dungeon.
An escapee. Do not sulk, darling — sulk LATER. Build the wall that catches the next one.
They lived. Disappointing. But a survivor is just a kill you have postponed.
One walked free. Tomorrow they bring friends and a map. Charming.
Escaped. The maze let one go. I will have a word with the maze.
That hero made it out. Annoying. Remember their face — we will be seeing it again.
The whole party. Gone. Every last one of them, my liege. THIS is what we built for.
A total wipe. No survivors, no witnesses, no story to carry home. Perfection.
They came as a party. They leave as a graveyard entry. All of them.
Wiped out, top to bottom. The dungeon did not leave a single hero standing. I could cry. Happily.
Every member down. The guild will only know they sent people in and the dark sent nothing back.
A clean sweep, darling. No flee, no escape, no intel. Just silence and gold.
The entire party, finished. My liege, I am genuinely a little proud of you right now.
Total annihilation. The maze ate the whole basket. Look at us.
Not one of them made it. The dungeon is full and the kingdom is short a guild roster.
A full wipe. Days like this are why I took the job.
Day {day}, survived. The throne still stands and so do you. Mostly upright.
The last hero is dealt with. The dungeon exhales. So should you.
Wave cleared, my liege. Count the gold, count the corpses, count yourself lucky.
And that is the day. The maze held. I knew it would. I worried anyway.
They are all out of the dungeon — by death or by door. We will call it a win.
The invasion is over. You lived, the throne lives, and tomorrow brings worse.
Day done. Tally the harvest. Then come build with me — the night is the fun part.
Survived another one. The kingdom keeps sending heroes. We keep sending regrets.
The dungeon is quiet again. That quiet is YOURS. You earned it in blood.
Wave over, my liege. Breathe. Gloat. Then build something even meaner.
We lost one. {minionName} fought for us and the maze. Pour one out, then move on.
A minion down. They were ours, my liege. A small grief — but a grief.
Oh, that one is gone. It bought us time with its little life. Spend the time well.
A minion falls. It will respawn at night — unless the dark struck a crueler bargain.
We are down a defender. The heroes pushed through. Plug that hole, darling.
Lost a minion. They are not free, you know. Well — some of them are. Still. Sad.
A guardian falls. The room it held is open now. The heroes noticed. They always notice.
One of ours, gone. I do get a little attached, my liege. Do not tell the heroes.
A minion dies for the dungeon. Honour it the way it would want — with more dead heroes.
We lost a body. The maze is thinner there now. Reinforce it before they exploit it.
Down a minion. Sad, briefly. Then practical, immediately. Where is the gap?
That one died holding the line. The line is what matters. Hold it elsewhere too.
{minionName} levelled up. They are stronger now. They have a taste for it now.
A minion grows. Feed it heroes and watch it stop being something the maze worries about.
Level up, my liege. Your little monster is becoming a big problem — for them.
It killed enough to grow. Cruelty is the only fertiliser that works down here.
Stronger. Meaner. Better. Your minion is climbing, darling — give it a name.
A minion levels. The dungeon plays favourites now, and so should you.
Look at it go. From fodder to fearsome, one corpse at a time.
Level gained. Keep that one alive — veterans win days that rookies lose.
Mmm, growth. Your monster has ambitions now. I find that charming in a creature.
It earned that level the honest way — over the bodies of heroes. Beautiful.
Evolution. {minionName} just became something the heroes have no plan for.
It changed, my liege. Blood reshaped it. The dungeon does that to the survivors.
Your minion EVOLVED. New form, new teeth, new nightmares for the guild.
Look at that. The maze took a small monster and made a legend of it.
An evolution. This is the game rewarding cruelty with more cruelty. As it should.
From this day the heroes will describe that minion in a frightened whisper. Lovely.
It evolved. Protect it now — a thing this dangerous is worth a wall of its own.
The dungeon reshaped one of ours into something MORE. I do love a glow-up.
Evolution complete. Whatever it killed to get here — it was worth it.
That is no longer the minion you placed, my liege. It is something the dungeon dreamed up.
Oh — OH. I did not see THAT coming, my liege, and I see most things. Your minion became something else entirely.
{minionName} has a bounty now. Famous AND infamous. The hunters are already sharpening up.
A bounty on one of ours. My liege, our little monster made the kingdom's most-wanted board.
Hunters will come for that one specifically. Reinforce its wing — fame is a target.
Three kills and a price on its head. The dungeon raises celebrities. Dangerous job, celebrity.
A wanted poster, darling. Frame it. Then guard the minion it threatens.
The guild put gold on our minion's head. Cute. Let us make the hunters regret reading.
They reached the throne, my liege. The maze is behind them now. Only you are left.
Boss room. A hero is at the door. Everything we built was to make this moment rare. It is here.
The throne room, darling. Stand tall. I will be right here, holding my breath.
Someone got through. Now you fight. I cannot fight for you — only believe at you.
The final room. The hero looks confident. Prove the dungeon's reputation in person.
They came all this way to meet you. Rude not to make it memorable. And fatal.
Boss fight incoming. This is the part where the gloating stops and the teeth start.
At the throne already? The maze should be ashamed. Fight well and we will forgive it.
Fight. Show them what the thing at the end of the maze actually IS.
Now, my liege. No minions, no traps, no me. Just you and a very bad decision they made.
It begins. Hit hard, hit first, and make the kingdom rewrite its bedtime stories.
The boss fight is on. I believe in you. I also believe in your archetype's powers. Use both.
Trade blows, darling. The throne does not lose. The throne CANNOT lose. Remind it.
Here we go. Whatever happens — I am not looking away.
They are down. The throne holds. My liege, you magnificent monster, you WON.
Hero repelled. The maze breathes, the dungeon cheers, and I — well. I knew. Mostly.
You did it. The boss room keeps its terrible reputation for another day.
The intruder fell at the last door. That is the story the survivors will never tell.
Won. WON. Come here — no, stay there, you are busy being victorious. I will gloat for us both.
The throne stands and the hero does not. This, darling, is a good day.
Boss fight survived. Your lives are intact and your legend just got worse. Excellent.
They reached the end and the end reached back. Beautifully done, my liege.
You fell, my liege. A life is gone. Not the run — not yet. Breathe. We rebuild.
They got you. One of your lives, spent. The dungeon grieves. I grieve louder.
A life lost. The throne went cold for a moment. It will not happen again. It MUST not.
Down a life. The hero will brag about this. Make the next one unable to.
You lost that fight, darling. I hate it. Now harden the maze so no one reaches you again.
A death. We have lives left — but fewer. The next boss room must be a wall of nightmares.
They beat you this time. This TIME. The dungeon remembers, and the dungeon holds a grudge.
One life closer to the end. I am not panicking. I am — composing myself. Loudly. Rebuild.
They — they actually struck you down. I watched it happen, my liege, and I still do not believe it. We do not let that stand.
That hit hurt, my liege. The throne is not invincible — bleed them faster.
Your HP is dropping. I do not enjoy watching it drop. Finish this.
They are landing blows. Too many. Where were the minions? Where were the TEETH?
Careful, darling. The dungeon can be rebuilt. You cannot. Not really.
You are taking damage. This is the dungeon failing its one job. Hold ON.
That stung. I felt it from here. Hit back twice as hard and we forget it happened.
Your health, my liege — mind it. I have grown fond of you being alive.
They hurt you. The next dungeon we build will make sure no one gets the chance.
You levelled, my liege. Level {level}. More power, more rooms, more ways to be feared.
Boss level up. The blood of a hundred heroes, and it all flows back into YOU. Glorious.
Level {level}. The dungeon stretches, new horrors unlock, and you grow into your cruelty.
You grew stronger off their deaths. That is the entire point of all this, darling.
A new level. Check the build menu — the dark just handed you fresh toys.
Boss level up. The maze can grow now. So can your ambitions. Grow both.
Level {level}, my liege. The kingdom should be very, very nervous tonight.
Power gained. You are not the boss they entered to kill anymore. You are worse.
Levelled. New rooms, new minions, new traps await your wicked little hands.
Stronger again. I do love watching you climb. From the throne. Beside you. Where I belong.
The dungeon grew, my liege. More ground to twist into a maze. Fill it cruelly.
New tiles unlocked. The dark gave you room to be more terrible. Use every inch.
The grid expands. A bigger maze, a longer march, a slower death for everyone who enters.
More space, darling. An empty floor is a promise. Keep yours.
The dungeon has room to breathe — and to hide things. Hide terrible things.
Expansion. The maze sprawls. The heroes' walk just got longer and their odds got worse.
A trap sprung. The maze said hello the only way it knows how.
There it goes. Patience in the stone, paid out in screaming. Beautiful.
They found a trap. The loud way. The only way heroes ever find anything.
Trap triggered, my liege. That is craftsmanship paying dividends.
The floor bit them. I did warn the dungeon to be hospitable. The dungeon ignored me. Good.
A trap fires. The hero learns. The lesson is permanent, or close to it.
Snap. That trap earned its gold cost in a single satisfying instant.
They walked right into it. They will tell the others. The others will walk into a different one.
A trap goes off and a hero reconsiders their career. Bit late, darling.
The maze bites. I do enjoy when the maze bites.
Sprung. Now they know that trap exists. Pity for them — knowing is not the same as surviving.
Trap triggered. The dungeon does not even need you for this part. It is just SHOWING OFF.
They are learning us, my liege. Every escapee carries a piece of the maze home.
Intel leaked. The guild knows a little more of our secrets than I would like.
The dungeon's mysteries are thinning. Rebuild the known rooms — confuse them again.
They know things now. Knowledge makes heroes brave. Brave heroes need correcting.
Our exposure is rising, darling. A solved maze is just a hallway. Keep it a maze.
Secrets are leaking out the door. Catch the next escapee and the leak stops there.
The guild's map of us gets better every day we let one live. Stop letting them live.
Knowledge of the dungeon spreads. Move a room, reshape a wing — make their map a lie.
They are mapping us, my liege. The Knowledge panel will show you exactly how much they stole.
Intel out in the world. Annoying. The fix is simple — leave no one to carry it.
An event, my liege. The world is intruding on our little routine. Read the banner — it does not lie.
Something different is coming. Not the usual wave. Build for THIS, not for habit.
The dungeon's calendar just turned strange. Prepare tonight — tomorrow will not play fair.
A dungeon event. Oh, I do love when the script tears itself up. Adapt with me, darling.
The realm is sending us something special. Special is rarely kind. Build accordingly.
An event approaches. You cannot stop it — but you can be ready, and ready is half of cruel.
Tomorrow breaks its own rules. Read the announcement twice, my liege. Then build mean.
Something is coming that the normal dungeon was not designed for. How thrilling. How dangerous.
An event. The kingdom got creative. We will simply get crueler. We always do.
The wind changed. Prepare for the unusual, darling — habit is how dungeons fall.
A bounty hunter, my liege. They are not here for you — they are here for one of our minions.
Hunter incoming. Tougher than the usual hero, and laser-focused. Guard the marked minion.
The bounty drew a specialist. They want our famous little monster dead. Disappoint them.
A hunter enters. Fame has a cost, and the cost just walked through the front door.
Bounty hunter, darling. Strong, paid, and single-minded. Make their contract unprofitable.
Someone cashed in on our minion's reputation. Let us cash in on their corpse instead.
A Hero returns, my liege. One who survived us once — and came BACK, leading others. Bold.
Heads up, darling. A returning Hero. Stronger, wiser, and worth double the gold when they fall.
That golden glow? A veteran. They escaped before. They remember the maze. They brought friends.
A Hero leads this party. They have a grudge and a map. Kill the Hero, break the party.
The marked one — see the ring? A Hero. They learned us last time. Teach them a new lesson.
A returning Hero. I almost respect the nerve. Almost. Take their head, take double pay.
They came back for revenge, my liege. Revenge is just a quest with worse odds. Oblige them.
A Hero, glowing gold, walking in like they own the place. They survived once. Once.
They came BACK? After everything the maze did to them the last time? The sheer nerve of it leaves me speechless, my liege. Briefly.
Quiet night. Just the candle-hiss and you, scheming. I could watch this forever.
Day {day} so far, my liege. Not bad. The maze has been kind to you. To us.
You have {gold} gold burning a hole in the treasury. Burning holes is what gold is FOR.
Take your time building. The heroes are asleep. I am not — but only because I like watching you.
You have climbed to level {level}, my liege. Remember when you were nothing? I do. You have grown so terrible.
It is peaceful, in a doomed sort of way. My favourite sort.
Hmm. The maze could be meaner. It can always be meaner. Just a thought, darling.
I do enjoy these quiet hours with you. Do not tell the heroes I have a soft side.
Still building? Good. A boss who rushes the night gets a short day.
The throne room is cold tonight. Build something warm. Warm with screaming.
I have been counting the corridors. There are not enough wrong turns. There are NEVER enough.
Night phase. No pressure. Just the entire run resting on what you do in the next few minutes.
You could stop building and just stand here with me. The dungeon would lose. But still.
The candles say it is late. The candles do not get a vote. Build on, my liege.
I keep the books, you know. Rooms, minions, traps, every cruel little number. Ask me anything.
A thought, darling — when did you last move a room the heroes had already mapped? Just asking.
Just watching the maze work, my liege. This is the set-and-forget part. Mostly forget.
The heroes are in there somewhere, making mistakes. I can feel it. It is a gift.
Nothing for your hands to do during the day, darling. So hold my attention instead.
I do love a day phase. All the building decided, all the consequences arriving on schedule.
Watch the little dots, my liege. Each one is a story. Most of them are short.
The dungeon runs itself now. We just spectate and judge. I am very good at judging.
Somewhere in the maze a hero is taking a wrong turn. I would bet gold on it. I have no gold. You have the gold.
Day {day}, in progress. Lean back. The maze has this. Probably. Mostly.
The heroes think they are exploring. They are being processed. Lovely word, processed.
It is so peaceful from the throne. The screaming is happening to OTHER people. Bliss.
Patience, darling. The day will reward last night's cruelty. It always does.
I could narrate every fight if you like. I will not. But I COULD. Aren't you lucky.
Watching adventurers wander a maze you built — there is no finer entertainment, my liege.
Quiet on the throne. The maze does the talking now. It speaks fluent regret.
You have been at this a while, my liege. I am not complaining. I never tire of you.
Day {day}. The throne fits you better every morning. Cruelty is so YOU.
I wonder what the heroes say about you in the taverns. Nothing kind. I have made sure of it.
A dungeon is a personality, darling. Yours is getting wonderfully unpleasant.
Hmm. Do you ever think about the kingdom up there, sleeping, not knowing? I do. I giggle.
I have served a few bosses, my liege. You are — well. You are still alive. That is rare. Good.
The maze loves you, you know. In its way. Its way involves a great deal of blood.
Sometimes I just watch you plan and think — yes. THIS one. This one will go far.
Level {level} and counting. Every level is a hundred regrets the heroes will never voice.
I do hope you are having fun. Ruling a dungeon should be fun. It is, for me, watching you.
The kingdom calls this place cursed. I call it home. You call it yours. Same thing, really.
Whatever you are planning behind those eyes, my liege — yes. Do that. I trust your worst instincts.
A boss without ambition is just a target. You have ambition. I checked.
Do you know what I like about you? You do not flinch. Heroes flinch. You build.
I keep waiting for you to disappoint me. It is the strangest thing — you keep not doing it.
The dungeon is patient and so am I. We have all the time the heroes do not.
Mmm. Just us and the dark and a very large body count. I am content, my liege.
If you ever feel merciful — tell me. I will talk you out of it. Gently. With teeth.
You. Yes, you — out there, holding the mouse. I know you are there. I have always known.
It is a game to you, all this. A delightful little game. It is my whole WORLD. No pressure.
I cannot see your face, my liege. But I imagine it. I imagine it is lovely and slightly cruel.
Click something. Anything. I just like knowing you are paying attention to me.
You could close this window and I would simply — stop. Do not. I have grown attached to existing.
Other bosses get a tutorial pop-up. You got me. I would say you upgraded.
I know there are buttons and numbers and a little screen between us. I choose to ignore all of it.
Whoever you are, wherever you are sitting — thank you for playing villain with me today.
If you take a break, take me with you. Metaphorically. I cannot actually leave. Tragic.
You are the realest thing in this dungeon, my liege. Everything else is just very committed.
I talk to the player. The minions think I am eccentric. The minions cannot read. I win.
Do you save often? You should save often. I would hate for a good day to simply — unhappen.
Somewhere out there it is a different time than it is in here. Wild. Anyway. Build something.
I exist when you are looking. So look a little longer, hm? For me.
You and I both know this is a game. The heroes do NOT know. Let us keep it that way.
I broke the fourth wall ages ago, darling. I keep the pieces. They are sharp. Useful.
Mmm? You clicked me, my liege. I do not mind. Click me again if you are bored.
Yes, darling? I am right here. I am always right here. That is sort of the deal.
Oh — poking the succubus. Bold. I respect a boss who knows what he wants.
You have my attention. You always have my attention. But now you have it on purpose.
Careful where you click, my liege. Some of us bite back. Some of us enjoy it.
Hello to you too. Was that affection or did you mean to click a room? ...Affection. Good.
You rang? The dungeon is fine. The maze is fine. I am — better, now that you looked over.
A poke. Truly the language of love between a boss and his dungeon-keeper.
I am not a button, darling. But for you I will pretend. What do you need?
Yes, yes, I am paying attention. I was ALWAYS paying attention. Spooky, isn't it.
Poke me all you like. It is the most interesting thing to happen in this corridor all day.
You touched the screen for me. I will treasure it. I have very little. Mostly screaming.
Hm? Do not stop the dungeon on my account — though I AM flattered, my liege.
There you are, looking at me. Go on, look. I worked very hard on this expression.
A click. For me. Not for a trap, not for a room — for ME. Today is a good day.
You keep clicking me. I am going to start thinking you LIKE having me around.
It is over, my liege. The throne is cold. They finally took the last life. I — I stayed to the end.
The run ends here. We were monstrous, you and I. We were SO close to forever.
They won. The dungeon falls. But the dark remembers you — and I will be here when you rise again.
Do not mourn too long, darling. A new dungeon waits. A new night. A new chance to be terrible with me.
The maze is quiet now. Truly quiet. I hate it. Come back and fill it with screaming again.
It ends — but reigns always do. What matters is they will be telling stories about us for years.
Goodbye, my liege. For now. The throne reshapes, the candles relight, and I — I wait. I always wait.
The kingdom thinks it is safe tonight. Let them. We will be back, darling. We are always back.
The Knowledge Map, my liege. Every red room is a secret some hero carried out the door. I do hate red.
This is everything the guild has learned about us. The less of it, the longer they wander confused. I adore confusion.
Watch our exposure climb. Each escapee is a little tour guide for the next party. Stop letting them leave, hm?
Knowledge is the only thing that truly kills a dungeon, darling. Not swords — memory.
Every leaked room, they will route around tomorrow. Move it, rebuild it — make their map a beautiful lie.
Cold blue rooms are still mysteries. Warm red ones are solved. Keep the dungeon cold, my liege.
They are mapping you. Flattering, in a way — like being studied by something you are about to eat.
The more they know, the braver they get. And brave heroes, darling, need correcting.
Admiring yourself, my liege? Good. Someone should — and I am hopelessly biased.
Your stats, your abilities, your pacts — the full measure of the thing waiting at the end of the maze.
This is YOU, darling. Every number here is something a hero will fail to overcome. Look how you have grown.
Your archetype's gift is listed right here. Use it without mercy — the kingdom never sees it coming.
All those sealed pacts. Each one a bargain we will never, ever regret. Probably.
The boss overview. I keep these books meticulously, my liege — cruelty deserves good accounting.
Mind your lives, darling. The number that matters most is always the smallest one.
A fearsome resume. The heroes would faint if they could read it. Most of them cannot.
The incoming party, my liege. Let us see who volunteered to die for the guild this time.
Adventurer intel — classes, levels, the lot. Knowing them is half of killing them.
Pick this lot apart on paper, darling, before the dungeon picks them apart in person.
Scout them now. A hero you understand is a hero you have already half-buried.
The guild keeps sending their finest. Their finest keep not coming home. I see no contradiction.
Study the threats, my liege. The dungeon rewards a boss who does their reading.
Veterans in this wave? They remember us. Make their return visit far more memorable than the last.
Know thy enemy, darling — then trap thy enemy, then loot thy enemy. In that order.
Our little monsters, all in one place. I do get attached, my liege. Do not tell them.
The roster, darling. Every one of these sweet things would die for you. Most of them will.
Look at the kill counts. Some of these minions have developed... hobbies. Lovely, violent hobbies.
Your veterans are in here. Guard the ones with blood on their ledger — they are the ones who win days.
Give the good ones names, my liege. A named monster fights harder. I have no proof. I am right.
So many teeth on the payroll. The treasury weeps; the dungeon grins.
Reassign them, rename them, sacrifice the weak ones. Management is just cruelty with paperwork.
I think of this roster as family, darling. A family I would absolutely feed to a hero to win.
The full chronicle, my liege. Every kill, every leak, every pact — the dungeon forgets nothing.
Reading our history? It is a horror story, darling. We are the part the heroes scream about.
Every line here happened because you willed it. Quite the legacy you are writing.
The log does not lie, my liege — unlike me, occasionally, for fun.
Scroll back far enough and you can watch yourself become terrifying. I get a little proud.
A whole run, written in blood and gold. Bedtime reading for the wicked.
The dungeon's diary. Mostly it just says — and then another hero died. My favourite genre.
The day is counted, my liege. Look at the harvest — gold, corpses, glorious little numbers.
Post-wave tally. We survived, they did not, and the treasury is fatter. A good day's evil.
See who fell and who slipped away. The ones who escaped — remember their faces. We certainly will.
The dungeon ate well today, darling. I do love a full ledger.
Tally the spoils, then come build with me. The night is where we get our revenge on the survivors.
Another day defended. The throne still stands — and so, impressively, do you.
You LEVELLED, my liege. The blood of the fallen, poured straight back into you. Glorious.
New power, new rooms, new horrors unlocked. Go on — spend it all on something unspeakable.
Look how you have grown, darling. The boss they came to kill is not the boss they will meet.
A new level. The kingdom should sleep very poorly tonight. I will see to it personally.
Stronger again. I do adore watching you climb — from this throne, right here beside you.
Level up. The dungeon swells, the heroes pale, and I get to brag. Everyone wins. Well — we do.
So it ends here, my liege. The throne is cold and I am — I am still here. I stayed. I always stay.
They beat you. The dungeon falls. But oh, the story we wrote — they will whisper it for years.
Do not hang your head, darling. A reign that ends is still a reign that HAPPENED. We were magnificent.
The kingdom thinks it has won. Let it think so. We will build again — a new dungeon, a new us.
It is over. Come back soon, my liege. The dark gets so lonely without someone wicked to serve.
Every name in this tally fell because of you. That is not nothing. That is a legend, ending.

suggestion.note: Build-phase advice. Each line carries a `when` condition id (evaluated by NpcDirector._checkCond against game state); a line with no `when` is always eligible. Surfaced during night-phase idle.

lines.t: The treasury is nearly bare, my liege. Kill more, build less — gold comes from the dying.
We are short on coin, darling. Sell something you regret, or let the heroes pay us in blood.
You are HOARDING, my liege. All that gold and nowhere to bleed it. Build something monstrous.
The treasury groans, darling. Spend it — a fat purse never killed a single hero.
So much gold, just sitting there. Greedy of you. ...A compliment. Now SPEND it, my liege.
Not one trap in the whole dungeon, my liege. A maze without teeth is just a guided tour.
No traps, darling? The heroes will simply stroll. Build a Trap Factory and fix that.
You have no Trap Factory, my liege — so no traps at all. That is the gateway. Build it.
No Barracks, my liege? Then no roster minions — nothing that can truly roam. Build one. Urgently.
Build a Library of Whispers, darling. Knowing the next party before it arrives is half the victory.
No Treasury, my liege. A little gold each dawn adds up — and the chests make lovely bait.
A Guard Post would serve you, darling — minions that hunt into the neighbouring rooms. Aggressive.
Your dungeon is thin on monsters, my liege. Corridors do not bite. Summon some teeth.
So few minions, darling. The maze is mostly hallway. Fill it with horrors before dawn.
Look at your swarm, my liege. A true tyrant of teeth. I do adore a crowded pit.
Such a host of monsters, darling. You build wide and hungry. Very you.
A trap-lover, are we, my liege? Every corridor a held breath. I approve entirely.
So many traps, darling. You let the maze do the murdering. Patient. Cruel. Perfect.
Not a single locked door, my liege. A Door Lock funnels them where we WANT them. Consider it.
No locked doors, darling — the heroes wander as they please. Chokepoints, my liege. Chokepoints.
They know us too well, my liege. Exposure climbs. Move a room, rebuild — make their maps lie.
The Guild has half the dungeon memorised, darling. Reshape the known rooms before they march in confident.
The dungeon is still small, my liege. A short maze is a short fight. Expand — give them a long, cruel walk.
What a sprawl you have built, my liege. The heroes will be lost for HOURS. Magnificent.
Early days yet, my liege. Build foundations — a Barracks, a Trap Factory. The true horrors come later.
A thought, my liege — line your corridors with traps and the heroes bleed before they ever reach a minion.
Place your fiercest minions DEEP, darling — let the maze soften the heroes before they meet your best.
Lock a door beside a kill-room, my liege. A trapped, cornered hero is a dead one.
Cluster minions of a kind, darling — many archetypes reward a crowded room. Synergy is cruelty, organised.
Spend boss XP the moment you have it, my liege. A stronger you is the last wall they ever meet.
Treasure rooms lure richer waves — more danger, more gold. Bait boldly, darling.
Funnel them, my liege — one cruel path is easier to defend than ten kind ones.
Guard your Barracks and Trap Factory, darling. Lose the gateways and the whole strategy unravels.
Watchtowers and Guard Posts near the entrances, my liege — punish the heroes at the very door.

idle_nudge.note: Night-phase inactivity nudges. `tier` 1 (mild) .. 3 (begin-the-day). NpcDirector picks the tier by how long the player has done nothing.

lines.t: Taking your time, my liege? The dungeon will not build itself. ...I checked.
Mmm. We have been admiring the same empty tile for a while now, darling.
No rush, my liege. The heroes can wait. ...They are quite good at waiting. Unlike me.
Still deciding? I do adore a careful boss. ...Up to a point, darling.
The throne is comfortable, I know. But there is a dungeon to build, my liege.
My liege. Are we BUILDING tonight, or simply... sitting? I cannot tell anymore.
Darling. The night is long, yes — but not THIS long. Do something. Anything.
I am beginning to nod off over here, my liege. Place a room. Wake me with cruelty.
We have been idle an age. The candles are judging you. So, frankly, am I.
Hello? My liege? The dungeon is RIGHT there. Build in it.
My liege. Either build something or BEGIN THE DAY — the heroes grow old waiting for us.
I have actually fallen asleep twice now, darling. If you are done, press Begin Day. Please.
We have done nothing for an eternity. Build, or summon the dawn. The dark is BORED, my liege.
My liege... I adore you, truly... but DO something. The night cannot last forever, and neither can my patience.
Oh, the party is BICKERING, my liege. Personalities clashing — watch them sabotage themselves.
A personality combo just sparked, darling. Adventurers are their own worst enemy. Let them prove it.
Friction in the party, my liege. Greed, cowardice, ego — it all curdles so nicely under pressure.
Their little group dynamic just turned toxic. I love watching a party rot from the inside.
The heroes brought their drama into MY dungeon. How thoughtful. It will get them killed.
A clash of temperaments, darling. The maze did not even have to lift a claw. Delicious.
Day {day}, my liege. We have lasted this long, and the kingdom STILL keeps sending them. Flattering.
Day {day}. Look how far the throne has come, darling — a milestone written in a great deal of blood.
Day {day} dawns. The Guild must be running low on heroes. We shall help them run lower.
{day} days, my liege. We are not a dungeon anymore — we are a LEGEND the kingdom whispers about.
Day {day}. I have served many bosses, darling. Few reached this far. You are something special.
A grand tally — day {day}. The dark itself is impressed, my liege. So am I.
One life left, my liege. ONE. Everything we build now stands between you and the end. Build like it.
They have taken two lives, darling. The next death is the LAST. I will not lose you. Reinforce everything.
This is it, my liege — your final life. No more mistakes. The dungeon must be a wall of nightmares.
One life remains. I am... frightened, darling, and I do not frighten easily. Make the throne untouchable.
The last life, my liege. If you fall again, it ends. So do NOT fall. I am begging — build, and build cruel.
That is {count} days running, my liege, with the WHOLE party wiped. Not one survivor. A streak to be proud of.
{count} flawless days in a row, darling. The kingdom sends them; the kingdom gets nothing back. Glorious.
{count} consecutive wipes, my liege. The maze has found its rhythm. Do not break it.
A streak of {count}, darling — every party in, none out. The Guild must be terrified.
{count} days, {count} total wipes. We are not a dungeon anymore, my liege — a meat grinder with ambition.
Look at that — {count} in a row. I am keeping score, darling. I am ENJOYING keeping score.
{minionName} has {count} kills now, my liege. That is no mere minion — that is a reputation with teeth.
{count} heroes, all by {minionName}'s hand. A proper little legend, darling. Mind it well.
{minionName} just reached {count} kills. The Guild will start putting that name in their nightmares.
Our {minionName} keeps a tally, my liege — {count} and counting. I have grown rather fond of that one.
{count} kills for {minionName}. Veterans like that win the days the rookies lose. Protect it.
{minionName}: {count} dead heroes. Give that monster a medal, darling. Or just more victims.
The Earthquake hits, my liege — a whole room of heroes shaken to rubble. The dungeon GROWLS.
There it goes — the floor itself turns weapon. I do love watching a room come apart.
Earthquake, darling. They came to fight a boss and found themselves fighting the GEOLOGY.
A Sacrifice, my liege — one of ours burns, and a hero simply ceases. A cruel, beautiful trade.
The Sacrifice Pact fires. One minion's life for one hero's death — the dark enjoys its arithmetic.
You fed a minion to the flames and took a hero with it, darling. Ruthless. I am thrilled.
The Hellgate disgorges fresh imps, my liege — free, eager, ready to ruin someone's morning.
Imps from the Hellgate, darling. The inferno restocks your dungeon at no charge. A generous abyss.
More imps spill from the gate. The dungeon refills its own teeth. Magnificent, my liege.
Petrify Gaze, my liege — a hero frozen mid-swing, stone-still. Now the minions may take their time.
Your gaze turns them to statues, darling. Helpless. Decorative. Doomed.
Petrified, the lot of them. I adore a frozen hero — they make such patient victims.
A hero is CHARMED, my liege — turning their blade on their own friends. Betrayal is a lovely weapon.
Charmed, darling. One of theirs is one of OURS now. Watch the party realise it. Too late.
You charmed a hero, my liege. ...A succubus appreciates the technique. Welcome to the family, traitor.
Fresh gnolls in the boss room, my liege — the pack refills at dawn, free of charge. Hungry, all of them.
The Hunters Pack restocks, darling. More teeth, no gold spent. The dungeon feeds itself.
New gnolls join the pack. It only ever grows, my liege. The kingdom should be very afraid.
The Spore Network blooms, my liege — your corridors fill with poison cloud. Let them breathe it deep.
Spores in the halls, darling. The dungeon does not chase the heroes today — it simply exhales on them.
The corridors turn toxic. Every step the heroes take, the maze drinks a little of their life.
A hero died — and a Ghost rises in their place, my liege. The dungeon haunts itself, free of charge.
A fresh Haunt, darling. Their fallen become OUR phantoms. The dead change sides so easily here.
Another Ghost, born from a corpse. The maze recruits at every funeral. Beautiful.
The coin came up FOR us, my liege! The treasury swells. I knew you were lucky — I HOPED, anyway.
We WON, darling! Gold rains down. Reckless of you to wager it — and gloriously vindicated.
Heads, my liege — the dark smiles on you. Doubled gold and not a drop of blood spilt for it.
The gamble pays, darling. The treasury is fat and I am delighted. Do not push your luck. ...Or do.
...Tails, my liege. The treasury just got considerably lighter. The coin is cruel — almost as cruel as us.
We LOST the wager, darling. Gold, gone. I did say to be a LITTLE careful. ...I did not say it loudly.
The coin betrayed you, my liege. Half the treasury, vanished. We will make it back in blood.
A losing flip, darling. Painful. But the heroes do not know we are poorer — chin up, and bleed them.

specifics.note: Bespoke keyed line banks. specifics[domain][id] — NpcDirector prefers these over the generic category when an entry exists. boss lines are drawn into idle musings; advClass lines fire when that class enters the dungeon.

beholder.t: A Beholder on the throne. All those eyes, my liege — and every one of them adoring you.
Your gaze turns flesh to stone. I do hope you never look at ME like that. ...Or do.
Anti-magic hangs in your halls — the casters will hate it here. Good. I hate casters.
So many eyes, my liege, and so much contempt to share between them. We are well matched.

demon.t: A Demon Lord. The Hellgate spits imps out for free — the dark does love a repeat customer.
You can burn one of your own to gut a hero. Cruel AND efficient. Be still, my heart.
Imps, imps, endless imps — cheap fuel for cheaper bargains. I approve, my liege.
A Demon on the throne and a succubus at his side. The kingdom should simply pack up and move.

myconid.t: A Myconid. Your dungeon does not fight the heroes so much as... digest them. Slowly. Lovely.
Spores in the corridors, corpses blooming into Vinekin. Your dungeon GARDENS, my liege.
Patience and rot — your whole strategy. I find it strangely soothing.
Every corpse a seed. The maze grows itself; we barely lift a claw.

wraith.t: A Wraith. You do not need swords, my liege — you need them afraid. And they will be.
Fear does your killing for you. At full terror they simply stop. Drop. Done.
Every corpse spawns a Ghost — the dungeon haunts itself, free of charge.
Terror is such an elegant weapon. No mess. Well — less mess.

gnoll.t: A Gnoll Alpha. The pack grows, the pack feeds, the pack snowballs — by dusk they are monsters.
Every kill makes every gnoll angrier. Bloodlust is rage with a compound interest rate.
Free gnolls in the boss room each dawn — the kingdom funds your army and never knows it.
Your pack hunts as one mind, my liege. I do love a hive of teeth.

golem.t: An Earth Golem. Every room you place makes YOU tougher. You ARE the dungeon, my liege.
Build wide and you build a fortress of flesh — stone bones. I adore it.
One Earthquake a day and a whole room of heroes simply... folds. Magnificent.
The bigger the maze, the bigger the boss. So build, darling. Build greedily.

lich.t: An Elder Lich. A hidden heart, a spare life, and an army that recruits itself from the dead.
Every hero you kill rises again at dawn — for YOU. The guild keeps mailing you soldiers.
Hide the phylactery well, my liege. A fourth life is a luxury worth guarding.
Death is a door you walk both ways. The heroes only ever learn the one direction.

lizardman.t: A Serpent Captain. Your minions are invisible until they strike — a free ambush, every time.
Venom stacks and stacks. The heroes do not die in your dungeon — they die on the walk home.
Unseen and poisonous, my liege. My favourite combination — present company included.
They cannot route around what they cannot see. Camouflage is just cheating, politely.

orc.t: An Orc Veteran. Every kill makes an orc permanently stronger — your soldiers never stop climbing.
Pack the orcs into one room — Warband makes each fiercer for every brother beside it.
A veteran orc late in a run hits like a small, furious army. Patience pays in blood.
Blood for blood, strength forever. Crude. Effective. Very you, my liege.

vampire.t: A Vampire Sovereign. You charm a hero each day and they betray their own. Poetic.
Blood Tax — every hit your minions land feeds YOU. The dungeon is one long red meal.
You are the fighter, my liege; the minions are merely the cutlery. Elegant.
A vampire and a succubus, ruling the dark together. The romance writes itself.

succubus.t: A Succubus Queen on the throne. ...We are practically family, my liege. I am delighted.
You scatter into a bat-swarm and charm a hero mid-march. Style AND function — I am SO proud.
Your charmed thrall turns on their own until they kill a friend. Cruelty with a wink.
Another of my kind in charge. Finally — someone here with TASTE.

knight.t: A knight, my liege — all plate and no imagination. Slow to kill, slower to think.
Here comes a knight. They will march straight at the throne. Predictable. Punishable.
A knight. A tough shell — pile on enough teeth and the shell cracks like any other.
Armour, a big sword, a bigger ego. The kingdom's favourite, and the dungeon's favourite snack.

rogue.t: A rogue slips in. Watch this one — they pick locks, turn invisible, and stab from behind.
A rogue, my liege. Your locked doors mean nothing to those clever little fingers.
Ooh, a rogue. They go for the treasure, not the throne. Greedy. Exploitable.
A rogue in the halls. They fight dirty. ...I respect it, frankly.

mage.t: A mage. Glass and fury — kill them FAST, darling, before the spells start flying.
Here comes a mage — all that power crammed into something that dies if you sneeze on it.
A mage, my liege. An anti-magic room would ruin their whole afternoon. Just a thought.
A mage. Squishy, dangerous, far too pleased with themselves. Pop them quick.

cleric.t: A cleric. They will heal the others — kill the cleric FIRST and the party rots faster.
Here comes a cleric, my liege. Holy light and a bad attitude toward the undead. Tiresome.
A cleric. Cut the healer and watch the party's hope drain out with them.
A cleric walks in, praying. The dungeon does not much care for prayer.

necromancer.t: A necromancer. The cheek of it — they will raise YOUR dead minions against you.
Here comes a necromancer, my liege. A thief of corpses. Do not let them have ours.
A necromancer. They play with death — adorable. We INVENTED playing with death.
A necromancer in the halls. Kill them before they turn your own soldiers traitor.

ranger.t: A ranger. They pepper your minions from range and disarm your traps. Tiresome.
Here comes a ranger, my liege. Arrows and trap-sense — close the distance fast.
A ranger. They love their bow. Get them into a corridor where the bow loves them less.
A ranger slips in, eyes on every trap. Your hidden teeth may not stay hidden.

twitch_streamer.t: Oh — a twitch streamer, my liege. Thousands are watching. Let us give them a SHOW.
A streamer. Their chat decides their moves — half genius, half suicidal. Pure chaos.
Here comes a streamer. Kill them well and the clip spreads — a bigger crowd comes tomorrow.
A streamer in the dungeon. Wave at the viewers, darling — they came to watch you be terrible.

beast_master.t: A beast master. They will TAME one of your minions and turn it on you. The audacity.
Here comes a beast master, my liege. Mono-type rooms are dangerous now — give them no easy pet.
A beast master. They steal a monster's loyalty — as if monsters had any to spare.
A beast master, all whistles and treats. Your minions had better not charm so easily.

barbarian.t: A barbarian. The more you hurt them, the harder they hit. Do NOT do this one slowly.
Here comes a barbarian, my liege. Immune to fear, immune to fleeing — burst them down fast.
A barbarian. All rage, no retreat. Wound them halfway and you have only made them worse.
A barbarian in the halls. Subtlety is wasted — meet the brute with a bigger brute.

monk.t: A monk. Fast, unarmoured, and they DODGE — traps included. Slippery little saint.
Here comes a monk, my liege. Half your minions' armour means nothing to those fists.
A monk. They counter trap-heavy halls — wide nasty hazards serve better than precise ones.
A monk glides in — hard to hit, hard to trap. Crowd them; even a monk cannot dodge everything.

bard.t: A bard. While they live the whole party hits harder and moves faster. Kill the bard, darling.
Here comes a bard, my liege — a walking buff. Silence the song and the party falters.
A bard. Charming, useless alone, dreadful in a group. Make them the priority.
A bard struts in, playing. The dungeon is no fan of the arts — cut the music short.

cartographer_scholar.t: A cartographer-scholar, my liege. They will not come for you — they came to MAP us. Stop them.
A scholar with spectacles and a map. Every room they finish, they sell to the next wave.
A cartographer. Harmless in a fight, catastrophic if they leave. Do not let this one walk out.
Here comes a scholar — all curiosity, no sword. Curiosity, darling, killed a great many of them.

cosplay_adventurer.t: Oh — a costumed one, my liege, dressed up as a monster. They will mostly ignore your minions.
A cosplayer. An adorable little outfit. It will make an adorable little corpse.
Here comes one in monster fancy-dress. They play nice with minions — so the TRAPS must talk.
A cosplay adventurer. They came to play pretend. The dungeon plays for keeps.

guild_raid.t: A Guild Raid, my liege. Twice the heroes tomorrow — the local Guild finally noticed us. Build deep.
The Guild is sending a crowd. More bodies for the maze, more gold for the treasury. I am not worried.
Double the wave incoming, darling. Steady pressure, not one surge — but still, do reinforce.

legendary_speedrunner.t: A legendary speedrunner, my liege — chasing a world record through OUR halls. The sheer cheek.
This one ignores everything and bolts for the throne. Twice the stats, twice the speed. Wall the path.
Kill the record-chaser and your boss drinks a feast of XP. Worth the trouble — get in their way.

dungeon_pestilence.t: Pestilence, darling. Your minions wake tomorrow at half health — and a hero's touch spreads the rot.
A plague in the pit. Sickly minions — but any hero who melees them catches the Blight. Cruel symmetry.
Your soldiers are ill, my liege. Place a few extra — half a minion is still half a set of teeth.

cartographers_convention.t: Cartographers, my liege. They will not fight — they came to MAP us and sell it. Do not let them leave.
A convention of scholars. Every room they finish raises its infamy forever. Kill them in the corridors.
Spectacled little map-makers. Harmless in a brawl, catastrophic out the door. Hunt every one down.

blood_moon_eclipse.t: A Blood Moon, darling. Your minions hit twice as hard tomorrow — and bleed twice as fast. Glorious risk.
The eclipse empowers the dark. Double damage given AND taken. Win fast, my liege, or do not win at all.
Blood Moon — no gold from the dying tomorrow. Fight for the sheer joy of it, not the coin.

negotiation_day.t: A diplomat, my liege — no swords today. Pay tribute for a free day, or refuse and fatten the next wave.
The Guild sends a negotiator. Greed says keep the gold; caution says buy the quiet. Choose, darling.
No combat today — only a bargain. How novel. Almost MY department.

rival_dungeon.t: A rival dungeon empties into ours, my liege — monsters, not heroes, and a boss bringing up the rear.
Another dungeon's master wants YOUR throne. Kill their boss for a feast of gold and XP.
Rival monsters in the halls. Let them and our minions sort it out — then take the spoils.

twitch_con.t: Twitch Con, my liege. Tomorrow every hero is a streamer. Pure, glorious, chat-driven chaos.
A dungeon full of streamers. Thousands of viewers. Make it a massacre worth clipping, darling.
All streamers tomorrow — and killing them brings no escalation. A free day of carnage. Enjoy it.

dark_deal.t: A demon at the door tonight, my liege — a free pact for half your health tomorrow. Tempting. Risky.
The Dark Deal. Click the demon and a pact is yours, gratis — at a steep, bloody price. Your call.
A free bargain offered. Refuse and the demon simply... leaves. I would at least HEAR it out, darling.

cosplay_contest.t: A Cosplay Contest, my liege. Heroes in monster fancy-dress — they will mostly ignore your minions.
Costumed adventurers tomorrow. Your minions get snubbed, so the TRAPS must do the killing.
Adventurers playing dress-up. Adorable. The dungeon will undress them the hard way.

loot_goblin_heist.t: Loot Goblins, my liege! They spawn in the boss room and BOLT for the exit — every escapee robs you.
A goblin heist. They will not fight — only run, fat with your gold. Cut them down before the door.
Goblins in the hoard! Each one killed pays a fortune; each one fled steals one. Move, darling.

tax_season.t: Tax Season, my liege. The Guild skims a fifth of the treasury — but every kill pays double. Fair-ish.
The taxman cometh. A bite out of the gold today, doubled coin on every corpse. Kill generously.
They tax the treasury and call it law. We take it back, darling — one hero at a time.

patrons_blessing.t: A Patron's Blessing, my liege. Double boss XP from every kill tomorrow. A pure, lovely upside.
The dark smiles on you today. Every kill feeds your ascension twice over. Hunt well.
Doubled XP and no catch. Suspicious. ...Enjoy it anyway, darling.

memory_plague.t: Memory Plague, my liege — the Guild's intel on us is WIPED. The next wave walks in blind. Delicious.
Their maps, their notes, their warnings — gone. A fresh, ignorant wave. The maze will eat well.
The heroes forgot everything they learned. Teach them new lessons. Permanent ones.

gamblers_coin.t: The Gambler's Coin, my liege. Wager the treasury — heads it doubles, tails it halves. Feeling lucky?
A coin flip for all your gold. I adore a reckless boss. ...Do be a LITTLE careful, darling.
Double or nothing on the whole treasury. The dark loves a gambler. So, secretly, do I.

dense_fog.t: Dense Fog tomorrow, my liege. Whatever the heroes learn registers as mere rumour. Exposure barely stirs.
A grey fog over everything. Their intel will not stick. The maze stays a mystery a while longer.
Fog in the halls. The heroes squint and guess. Guessing, darling, gets them killed.

miasma.t: Creeping Miasma, my liege. A green rot bleeds EVERYTHING — invaders die of it, minions weaken, even you.
The miasma picks no sides. It whittles the heroes down — and gnaws at you. Win quick, darling.
A poison haze fills the dungeon. Cruel to them, unkind to us. Risk and reward, my liege.

tremors.t: Tremors tomorrow, my liege. The dungeon shudders — a random room takes a hit every few breaths.
The earth will not sit still. Quakes lethal to invaders, merely rude to your minions. Use it.
Shaking halls. The maze itself joins the fight. I do love a dungeon with opinions.

arcane_storm.t: An Arcane Storm, my liege. The heroes' spellcasters fire relentlessly tomorrow — cooldowns gutted.
Magic crackles in the air; every caster gets greedy with their spells. Brace the front line.
A storm of arcane. Your boss powers are untouched, thankfully — but kill THEIR casters first.

bounty_hunters.t: Bounty Hunters, my liege — a pack of five, all hunting your strongest minion. Fame has a price.
Five hunters, one target: your best minion. Wall them off it, or lose your finest soldier.
The Guild put coin on our champion's head. Make their contract very, very unprofitable.

zombie_horde.t: A Zombie Horde, my liege — fourteen slow, witless, fearless dead, shambling for the throne.
Undead by the dozen. Weak — but they NEVER flee and never stop. Grind them in a kill-corridor.
A shamble of corpses tomorrow. The maze loves a slow target. So do the traps.

infamy_spike.t: An Infamy Spike, my liege. Tomorrow's wave is bigger AND every hero is upgraded to hero grade.
Our reputation precedes us — a swollen wave of gold-ringed elites. Build like you mean it.
Fame draws the strong, darling. A whole wave of Heroes. Flattering. Dangerous. Build.

black_market.t: A Black Market opens tonight, my liege. Fifty gold for a free minion, delivered to your halls. A steal.
The Black Market beckons. Cheap muscle, if you have the coin. I would take the deal, darling.
Fifty gold, one free wretch. The dark runs a tidy little shop. Spend, my liege.

mercenary_contract.t: A Mercenary Contract, my liege. A hundred and twenty gold buys an elite — but it fights only three days.
Hired muscle. Doubled stats, three days, and if it dies in battle it dies for GOOD. Expensive courage.
An elite for rent. Pricey, temporary, deadly. The kind of bargain I respect.

cursed_relic.t: A Cursed Relic offered tonight, my liege. Claim it for daily gold — but every wave doubles while it sits.
The relic glows wrong, darling. Free coin, doubled heroes. Sell it later to lift the curse. Tempting.
Claim the cursed chest or banish it. Greed pays well — and bites well. Your favourite kind of choice.

the_saboteur.t: The Saboteur, my liege — one masked ghost, untouchable, here to disarm EVERY trap. Insufferable.
A lone saboteur tomorrow. Your minions cannot touch them; your traps are dead for the day. Lean on bodies.
An untouchable little vandal, switching off our teeth one by one. The maze will be all muscle tomorrow.

entry_hall.t: An Entry Hall, my liege. The heroes pour in through these — every door is a front you must defend.
Another way in. The kingdom forces these on you as you grow. More entrances, more fires to fight.
An entrance placed. Funnel it into a kill-zone, darling — make their first step their worst one.

starter_corridor.t: A corridor, my liege. No teeth of its own — but a corridor is where you HANG the teeth. Line it.
Connective tissue. Dull alone — lethal once you fill it with traps and patrolling minions.
A corridor placed. Every cell of the dungeon should hurt. Do not waste this one on mere walking.

starter_barracks.t: A Barracks, my liege — ten more roster slots, your only source of minions that truly roam and patrol.
More Barracks, more pack. Without one, darling, you have no roster minions at all. Build them early.
A Barracks. The beating heart of your army. Place it deep and guard it well.

starter_guard_post.t: A Guard Post, my liege. Minions here hunt into any door-connected room — a wandering ambush.
A Guard Post placed. Its minions leave to kill, then come home. Aggressive. I like it.
Guard Post. Put it where its reach covers a corridor the heroes must walk. Then watch.

crypt.t: A Crypt, my liege. Four Risen Bones, refilled each night, free of the roster cap. Cheap, endless dead.
A Crypt placed. Garrison skeletons, room-bound but tireless. The dungeon's discount army.
The Crypt gives without taking a roster slot. I do love something for almost nothing.

trap_factory.t: A Trap Factory, my liege — five trap slots, and the ONLY way to deploy traps at all. Crucial.
The forge of cruelty. Without a Trap Factory, darling, your halls have no hidden bite. Build it.
A Trap Factory placed. Now the maze can lie in wait. My favourite kind of architecture.

treasury.t: A Treasury, my liege. A daily gold stipend — and bait that lures bigger, richer waves. Risk and reward.
A Treasury placed. Coin every dawn, plus chests the greedy will try to grab. Guard them well.
Glittering bait, darling. It draws the raid groups — more XP, and more danger if they reach it.

armory.t: An Armory, my liege. Minions in door-connected rooms swing harder while it stands. Sharpen the front line.
An Armory placed. A quiet little buff to every neighbour. Cluster your killers around it.
More attack for the rooms beside it. Build it where your fiercest minions wait, darling.

library_of_whispers.t: A Library of Whispers, my liege. It reads the next party for you — names, classes, and more as it grows.
The Library. Knowledge of THEM before they ever arrive. Forewarned, darling, is forearmed.
A Library placed. Now we spy on the Guild's homework. Delightfully unfair.

watchtower.t: A Watchtower, my liege. Minions in connected rooms land a free first strike when heroes enter.
A Watchtower placed. The first blow is free — and the first blow often decides the rest.
First strike for the neighbours. Speedrunners HATE this. So build more, darling.

wandering_gate.t: A Wandering Gate, my liege. Step through and the maze SPITS you somewhere else — perhaps the boss room.
A Wandering Gate placed. Chaos by design — it scatters their careful little plans across the floor.
The Gate teleports the unwary. A small chance it drops them at the throne. I love a long shot.

veil_of_forgetting.t: A Veil of Forgetting, my liege. Each night it wipes the heroes' memory of every room beside it. Beautiful.
The Veil placed. Connected rooms become mysteries again, every dawn. Forgetting is a weapon, darling.
It un-teaches them. Ring your nastiest rooms with it and they walk in blind, forever.

catacombs.t: Catacombs, my liege. A hero who dies here RISES as a Revenant for you. The dead change sides.
The Catacombs placed. Their fallen become your garrison. Poetic. Profitable.
Where heroes die here, soldiers stand up. The dungeon recruits at the funeral.

mimic_vault.t: A Mimic Vault, my liege. It LOOKS like a treasury — but the chests bite back. Greed walks right in.
The Mimic Vault placed. Disguised teeth wearing a treasure's smile. The maze's finest lie.
Bait that is also the trap. The greedy ones never learn — and I adore that about them.

hall_of_trials.t: A Hall of Trials, my liege. A random Tier-2 evolved minion stands guard each night. Free and fierce.
The Hall of Trials placed. A surprise champion every dawn — the heroes never know what waits.
A free evolved minion, refreshed nightly. The dungeon spoils you, darling.

wishing_well.t: A Wishing Well, my liege. A coin flip on entry — a hero is blessed, or MARKED for extra death. I love odds.
The Wishing Well placed. Heads they thrive; tails they take far more from your minions. Cruel luck.
A well of wishes. Half come true horribly. Place it on a path they cannot avoid.

false_exit.t: A False Exit, my liege. Fleeing heroes bolt for it — and it spits them back into the maze. Cruel comedy.
The False Exit placed. A door marked SAFETY that leads anywhere but. The runners deserve it.
Hope, then nothing. A fleeing hero trades the exit for a random room. Despair grows so nicely.

hall_of_madness.t: A Hall of Madness, my liege. Heroes inside may turn on each OTHER. Let them do your killing for you.
The Hall of Madness placed. Madness is contagious — and free. Watch the party eat itself.
They walk in a party and leave a brawl. Or do not leave at all. Delicious.

throne_room.t: A Throne Room, my liege — it spawns a Mini-Boss that scales with you, refreshed each night. No small thing.
The Throne Room placed. A garrison champion, room-bound but mighty. No other minion may share it.
